what_it_does: This file is a living harvest/retirement guide for fork lane ch-8djrpmsl, summarizing the session's perspective, concrete deliverables with honest verification status, learned bug-discovery heuristics, and a forward-handoff for the next bounce.
observations: It documents a cross-session fabric fork lane led by ch-3mpkjg3r and authored by ch-8djrpmsl.
It lists four concrete deliverables with explicit verification states such as VERIFIED, MODEL-LEG VERIFIED / LIVE ROUTE ATTEMPTED-UNVERIFIED, and DESIGN COMPLETE / BUILD NOT STARTED.
It references four source board items and four git commits as provenance.
It openly names gaps, notably that the L5 live HTTP route is attempted-unverified and not called done.
It records methodology claims like default-to-wrong catching false-greens and a friction-gap-sensor finding latent bugs.
It provides open questions and a forward-handoff tied to other sessions or people (recollection, Tim, chat-4b).
It follows a retirement protocol referenced by board://item-78c63045.
standouts: Explicit verification-state taxonomy rather than a simple done/not-done checklist.
Honest refusal to mark the L5 live route as verified.
Forward-handoff instructions that name external blockers and next-bounce actions.
Default-to-wrong and friction-gap-sensor described as deliberate bug-discovery practices.
conventions: YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, links, and history.
board:// URI scheme for internal item references.
Markdown body with section headers and bullet lists.
Session/channel identifiers (ch-...) and git commit hashes.
Honest-state / no-self-certify framing and retirement-protocol provenance.
concerns: Several deliverable states depend on external verification by other sessions or people, so the file cannot fully vouch for them.
Dense project-specific jargon and acronyms (L1–L5, resolve_slot, cog_run_role) may obscure meaning for readers outside the lane.
Commit and route claims are asserted without inline evidence, relying on linked items.
The distinction between committed and live is stated but not substantiated inside the file itself.
notes: A fork-lane retirement note that pairs an honest deliverable checklist with explicit gaps and a resumer handoff.
questions: What exactly distinguishes 'MODEL-LEG VERIFIED' from 'LIVE ROUTE ATTEMPTED-UNVERIFIED' in this context?
Which specific code changes correspond to commits 0d9ac76, a2c9e84, b3a2a79, and 61fed90?
What does the retirement protocol at board://item-78c63045 require?
Who or what are 'Tim', 'chat-4b', and 'recollection' in the forward-handoff?
What do 'L1–L5' and 'resolve_slot' refer to technically?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a perspective + coverage index + honest forward-handoff for the fork lane, and the file does exactly that, so they align.
apparent_themes: Fork-lane collaboration in a session fabric.
intention_signals: The file is trying to close out a session by capturing what was actually built, what remains uncertain, and what the next participant should do, while resisting the pressure to mark unfinished work as complete.
novelty: The explicit use of verification-state labels like 'MODEL-LEG VERIFIED · LIVE ROUTE ATTEMPTED-UNVERIFIED' and a 'default-to-wrong' bug-hunt methodology as first-class content in a retirement handoff.
contribution: It contributes a self-aware, state-labeled handoff artifact that preserves both accomplishments and open gaps, enabling a later session to resume without re-deriving what is and is not verified.
summary_for_embedding: A living harvest guide for fork lane ch-8djrpmsl that indexes session deliverables, verification states, learned bug-discovery heuristics, and forward-handoff tasks under a retirement protocol.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an honest, resumable close-out record for a fork lane so the next bounce can verify the unverified L5 route, build the pending dragnet, and reconcile dependencies with other sessions.
